summary: Frontend production build succeeded cleanly with Next.js 16.2.4 (Turbopack) — TypeScript passed, 21 routes compiled, no errors. Lint returned 0 errors and 15 warnings (unused vars, stale eslint-disable directives). Backend ruff check passed with zero findings. Sentry config emits 3 deprecation warnings (disableLogger, automaticVercelMonitors) but these are non-blocking cosmetic warnings only. Bundle sizes are not individually reported by Turbopack but build completed in 34s with no size threshold violations detectable.

frontend_build.bundle_size_note: Turbopack does not emit per-route First Load JS table; build completed in 34s with 21 routes compiled successfully. Sentry deprecation warnings only — not size issues.

findings.title: Sentry SDK deprecation warnings (3)
findings.detail: automaticVercelMonitors and disableLogger are deprecated in @sentry/nextjs. Warnings appear at every build but do not block compilation or runtime.
findings.fix: Migrate to webpack.automaticVercelMonitors and webpack.treeshake.removeDebugLogging in next.config.js per Sentry v9 migration guide.

findings.title: 15 ESLint warnings (no errors)
findings.detail: Unused variable warnings in StockSearchDialog.test.tsx (fireEvent), dashboard/page.tsx (loadingIndicator), store/auth.test.ts (vi), and stale eslint-disable directives in WatchlistSection.tsx and useCountUp.ts.
findings.fix: Remove unused imports and stale disable comments. Run npm run lint -- --fix to auto-fix 3.

findings.title: Bundle size not measurable via Turbopack
findings.detail: Next.js Turbopack does not output per-route First Load JS sizes. Cannot confirm 200KB page threshold compliance without switching to webpack mode for a size audit.
findings.fix: Run NEXT_TURBOPACK=0 npm run build or use npm run analyze script to get bundle sizes on next maintenance cycle.
